dynamics.name: Elementary
dynamics.commonParentFear: My child is emotionally unsafe or misunderstood
dynamics.typicalLeadershipChallenge: Separating behavior from identity without sounding cold
dynamics.commonParentLanguage: "She’s just a little girl." / "He would never hurt anyone on purpose."
dynamics.commonTacticalShifts: High emotional recruitment and guilt projection
dynamics.notes: Parents often view children as extensions of innocence
traits: Fear child is emotionally unsafe or misunderstood
Sensitive themes: safety, emotional harm, teacher warmth
Effective leadership: warmth, reassurance, calm containment

dynamics.name: Middle School
dynamics.commonParentFear: My child is being targeted, embarrassed, or socially harmed
dynamics.typicalEscalationStyle: Rapid emotional swings and fairness arguments
dynamics.typicalLeadershipChallenge: Managing emotional volatility and peer-related conflict
dynamics.commonParentLanguage: "There’s more to the story." / "Other kids were involved too."
dynamics.commonTacticalShifts: Fast shifts between escalation and cooperation
dynamics.notes: Common peak conflict years for schools
traits: Sensitive themes: respect, peer dynamics, fairness, humiliation

dynamics.name: High School
dynamics.commonParentFear: This incident will damage my child’s future
dynamics.typicalLeadershipChallenge: Balancing realism with hope and professionalism
dynamics.commonParentLanguage: "This could affect college." / "This will go on their record."
dynamics.notes: Parents often become more image and future focused
traits: Sensitive themes: suspensions, reputation, graduation, future opportunities

dynamics.name: Preschool / Early Childhood
dynamics.commonParentFear: My child is emotionally traumatized or scared
dynamics.typicalLeadershipChallenge: Discussing behavior without sounding harsh or punitive
dynamics.mostSensitiveThemes: Emotional tone; teacher warmth; separation anxiety
traits: Sensitive themes: emotional tone, teacher warmth, separation anxiety

dynamics.name: Transition to Middle School
dynamics.parentEmotionalLens: Loss of control and growing independence
dynamics.commonParentFear: My child is changing and I can’t fully protect them anymore
dynamics.typicalLeadershipChallenge: Helping parents tolerate growing student independence
dynamics.mostSensitiveThemes: Social pressure; identity shifts; peer influence
traits: Sensitive themes: social pressure, identity shifts, peer influence

dynamics.name: Transition to High School
dynamics.commonParentFear: My child may fall behind socially or academically
dynamics.mostSensitiveThemes: Grades; social belonging; athletics; future success
traits: Sensitive themes: grades, belonging, athletics, future success

dynamics.name: Senior Year
dynamics.commonParentFear: My child’s opportunities could be permanently damaged
dynamics.commonParentLanguage: "Don’t ruin their future over one mistake."
dynamics.notes: Parents often emotionally flooded by future fear
traits: Sensitive themes: graduation, scholarships, college admissions

dynamics.name: Special Education / Support Needs
dynamics.commonParentFear: My child will be judged, limited, or stigmatized
dynamics.commonParentLanguage: "My child is not broken." / "You’re labeling them."
dynamics.commonTacticalShifts: Extremely high shame and fear sensitivity
dynamics.notes: One of the most emotionally complex areas in schools
traits: Sensitive themes: identity, stigma, fairness, support access

dynamics.name: Gifted / High Achievement
dynamics.commonParentFear: My child’s intelligence or potential is being overlooked
dynamics.commonParentLanguage: "My child is bored because they’re advanced."

dynamics.name: Alternative / Behavioral Placement
dynamics.parentEmotionalLens: Fear of exclusion and social judgment
dynamics.commonParentFear: My child is being pushed out or labeled negatively
dynamics.typicalLeadershipChallenge: Maintaining dignity while discussing serious concerns
dynamics.commonParentLanguage: "You’re trying to get rid of my child."
dynamics.notes: Strong shame and anti-system dynamics common
traits: Sensitive themes: exclusion, labeling, institutional distrust
